title: First-feasible cumulative-guard line search
body: Let theta, m, and v be the frozen pre-attempt weights and AdamW moments, and let U(alpha) reapply the same moment update with learning-rate multiplier alpha. Q2.5 evaluates alpha in {1, 1/2, ..., 1/128} and commits the first U(alpha) whose immutable-baseline six-slice replay increase is finite and at most 0.015. Every trial starts from the same theta, m, and v; only the outer attempt advances sampling and attempt budgets.
claims: Descending first-feasible selection is deterministic and does not optimize against the public evaluator.
Restoring moments as well as weights makes each scale a trial of the same proposed AdamW update.
The immutable 1.5% authority retains the 0.5 percentage-point reserve below the public replay ceiling.
